title: Create division equations with area models
description: Practice using area models to solve division equations.
topic_path: Topic E: Division of tens and ones with successive remainders
Create division equations with area models

assessment_items.question_content:
Jerry split up the area of a rectangle to help him solve a division equation.

![area model split into 4 parts, each with a width of 6 units. From top to bottom the parts have a height of 100 units and an area of 600 square units; a height of 20 units and an area of 120 square units; a height of 4 units and an area of 24 square units; an unlabeled height and an area of 2 square units](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/550b73a82965707396fc889e8c1ab7551a58c2d2)

**What division equation did Jerry solve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\redE{\text{total area}}\div \tealE{\text{width}} = \maroonD{\text{height}}$

To find the area of the entire rectangle, we need to add the areas of the smaller rectangles that make it up.

$\purpleD{600}+\blueE{120}+\greenE{24}+\goldE{2}=\redE{746}$

The area of the entire rectangle is $\redE{746}$ square units.

 ![the same model with the combined area labeled 746 square units and the height labeled question mark units](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b97cc4bd6c274c50636f352b6c9ffa329f16ac7)

Jerry is dividing the area of the entire rectangle by $\tealE{6}$.  

To find the total height, we add each of the smaller heights. We know three of the heights.

$100+20+4=\maroonD{124}$ 

The leftover smaller rectangle has an area of $\goldE{2}$ square units. That is less than $\tealE{6}$, so it is Jerry's remainder.
Jerry was solving the equation $\redE{746} \div \tealE{6} =\maroonD{124}$ remainder $\goldE{2}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Aria split up the area of a rectangle to help her solve a division equation.

![area model split into 4 parts, each with a width of 2 units. From top to bottom the parts have a height of 4,000 units and an area of 8,000 square units; a height of 150 units and an area of 300 square units; a height of 1 unit and an area of 2 square units; an unlabeled height and an area of 1 square units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/053198f21b6e8bec2419a599a898ac39fe14e58b)

**What equation did Aria solve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\redE{\text{total area}}\div \tealE{\text{width}} = \maroonD{\text{height}}$

To find the area of the entire rectangle, we need to add the areas of the smaller rectangles that make it up.

$\purpleD{8{,}000}+\blueE{300}+\greenE{2}+\goldE{1}=\redE{8{,}303}$

The area of the entire rectangle is $\redE{8{,}303}$ square units.

 ![the same model with the combined area labeled 8,303 square units and the height labeled question mark units](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/342b00eda60b7baec58be525b7b84706d3f50e8d)

Aria is dividing the area of the entire rectangle by $\tealE{2}$.  

To find the total height, we add each of the smaller heights. We know three of the heights.

$4{,}000+150+1=\maroonD{4{,}151}$ 

The leftover smaller rectangle has an area of $\goldE{1}$ square unit. That is less than $\tealE{2}$, so it is Aria's remainder.
Aria was solving the equation $\redE{8{,}303} \div \tealE{2} =\maroonD{4{,}151}$ remainder $\goldE{1}$.

$\purpleD{500}+\blueE{100}+\greenE{50}+\goldE{45}=\redE{695}$

The area of the entire rectangle is $\redE{695}$ square units.

 ![the same model with the combined area labeled 695 square units and the height labeled question mark units](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/57ee5b671f70129e8f10f3feaa5bcb51108c5992)

![area model split into 2 parts, each with a width of 9 units. From top to bottom the parts have a height of 800 units and an area of 7,200 square units; a height of 54 units and an area of 486 square units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e1f0726f289f84d116c79d2624eb01e0a8336c8f)

To find the area of the entire rectangle, we need to add the areas of the smaller rectangles that make it up.

The area of the entire rectangle is $\redE{7{,}686}$ square units.

 ![the same model with the combined area labeled 7,686 square units and the height labeled question mark units](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/26c8aef73ca235dad5aca7eb31f3a7ed7c64adbd)

There is no leftover, so there is no remainder.
Valerina was solving the equation $\redE{7{,}686} \div \tealE{9} =\maroonD{854}$.
